what_it_does: Defines window.CV_MODE, an IIFE-wrapped registry of interaction MODES. Each mode declares what a click does, hover hints, cursor, accent, behaviour config flags, and lifecycle hooks (onEnter/onExit/onPick). Activating a mode reflects it onto document (data-mode attr, body cursor), pushes its behaviours to CV_AI as the active set, and runs setup/teardown. Ships two built-in modes: 'operator' (normal click navigates/edits) and 'inspect' (click any element to select it and hand to Vi). Defaults to operator on DOM ready.
observations: Same register/resolve/query/activate/subscribe API shape stated to mirror other registries
api object exposes levers to mode hooks: pick, stopPick, toast, openVi, setPicked — all guarded against missing globals (CV_PICK, dsaToast, ATOMICITY)
activate() throws on unknown mode id (loud fail), runs prev.onExit then next.onEnter
inspect mode re-arms pick in a 60ms setTimeout loop so selection is persistent until mode changes
config flags (clicksSelect, navTree, viContext) are declared as the single source for 'what's on'
describe() returns active id + modes list (id/label/config) for introspection
emit() wraps each listener in try/catch logging to console.error
standouts: interaction = f(mode) — the stated organizing equation: the interaction layer is a function of the active mode
Entering a mode pushes its behaviours into CV_AI so Vi's tone/permissions follow the mode (mode drives AI)
conventions: Follows the project's register/resolve/query/activate/subscribe registry contract
Loud-fail on unknown mode (throws) per the non-negotiables
Defensive optional-chaining guards for cross-module globals
Extend-by-registration: new modes drop in as data without touching the shell
concerns: Empty catch blocks (catch {}) around CV_AI.setActiveBehaviours and listener emit swallow errors silently, which is in tension with the loud-fail rule
setTimeout re-arm loop in inspect mode has no explicit cancellation token beyond the active()-id check
notes: The mode registry: pick OPERATOR or INSPECT (or register your own); the active mode decides what clicks do and reconfigures Vi's behaviour set.
questions: Where are CV_PICK, CV_AI, and ATOMICITY defined and what is their load-order relative to this file?
Are additional modes (annotate, measure, compare mentioned in the header) registered elsewhere?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — header docstring describes a mode registry driving interaction and Vi behaviours; code implements exactly that with the two stated built-ins.
apparent_themes: Single source for 'what's on' via config flags
Mode as a function over interaction
intention_signals: Tries to make interaction modal and data-driven so the meaning of a click is governed by one active mode, and to bind the AI assistant's tone/permissions to that same mode — one lever changes both interaction and AI.
novelty: Coupling an interaction-mode registry to an AI behaviour set (entering a mode reconfigures Vi) is the distinct move.
contribution: Contributes the modal-interaction layer: a registry that defines what a click means per mode and synchronizes the AI assistant's active behaviours to the active mode, with built-in operator/inspect modes and a document-level data-mode reflection for CSS.
summary_for_embedding: A JS IIFE registry (window.CV_MODE) of interaction modes — operator and inspect built-ins — where the active mode governs click behaviour, cursor, config flags, and the AI assistant's active behaviour set.
intention_for_embedding: To make interaction modal and single-sourced so one active mode decides what clicks do and simultaneously sets Vi's tone and permissions.
